name: GazeTracking
tagline: Lightweight Python library for real-time gaze tracking
short_impression: GazeTracking is a minimalist Python library built on OpenCV that provides simple gaze direction detection (left, right, center) and eye state tracking (open/closed). While not designed for precise coordinate tracking like research-grade systems, it offers an accessible entry point for developers needing basic eye tracking functionality in desktop applications.
pros: Simple API - only a few lines of code to get started
Lightweight and fast for basic gaze direction
cons: Limited to directional gaze (left/right/center), not precise coordinates
Not suitable for detailed fixation analysis
claimed_accuracy: Directional only (left, right, center) - not coordinate-based
privacy_notes: All processing occurs locally. No network requests or data transmission.
